content.thinking:
The openssl-toolbox has openssl but not cockroach. I'll need to use openssl to generate the certificates manually.

For CockroachDB TLS, I need:
1. CA cert (ca.crt) and CA key (ca.key)
2. Node cert (node.crt) and node key (node.key) - same cert used for all nodes with SANs for each pod
3. Client cert (client.root.crt) and client key (client.root.key)

The certificates need to be placed at `/cockroach/cockroach-certs/` in each pod.
I'll generate these step by step using openssl commands: first the CA key and certificate, then the node key and certificate signing request, sign it with the CA, and finally create the client root certificate. The node certificate needs Subject Alternative Names for all three pod DNS names plus the public service endpoint.

content.text:
The toolbox only has openssl. I'll generate the CockroachDB TLS certificates using openssl manually. Here's the plan:
1. Generate CA cert/key in the toolbox
2. Generate node cert (with all SANs) and client.root cert
3. Create Kubernetes secrets from the certs
4. Update the StatefulSet to use secure mode with cert mounts

Starting with certificate generation:

mkdir -p /tmp/certs /tmp/ca

openssl req -new -x509 -days 3650 -key /tmp/ca/ca.key \
  -out /tmp/ca/ca.crt \
  -subj '/O=Cockroach/CN=Cockroach CA'

echo 'CA generated'

" 2>&1

input.command:
kubectl -n cockroachdb exec openssl-toolbox -- sh -c "
# Generate node key and CSR with all SANs
openssl genrsa -out /tmp/certs/node.key 2048 2>&1

cat > /tmp/node-ext.cnf << 'EOF'
[req]
req_extensions = v3_req
distinguished_name = dn

[dn]

[v3_req]
subjectAltName = @alt_names
extendedKeyUsage = serverAuth, clientAuth

[alt_names]
DNS.1 = localhost
DNS.2 = crdb-cluster-0.crdb-cluster.cockroachdb.svc.cluster.local
DNS.3 = crdb-cluster-1.crdb-cluster.cockroachdb.svc.cluster.local
DNS.4 = crdb-cluster-2.crdb-cluster.cockroachdb.svc.cluster.local
DNS.5 = crdb-cluster.cockroachdb.svc.cluster.local
DNS.6 = crdb-cluster-public.cockroachdb.svc.cluster.local
DNS.7 = *.crdb-cluster.cockroachdb.svc.cluster.local
IP.1 = 127.0.0.1
EOF

openssl req -new -key /tmp/certs/node.key \
  -out /tmp/certs/node.csr \
  -subj '/O=Cockroach/CN=node' \
  -config /tmp/node-ext.cnf 2>&1

openssl x509 -req -days 3650 \
  -in /tmp/certs/node.csr \
  -CA /tmp/ca/ca.crt \
  -CAkey /tmp/ca/ca.key \
  -CAcreateserial \
  -out /tmp/certs/node.crt \
  -extensions v3_req \
  -extfile /tmp/node-ext.cnf 2>&1

echo 'Node cert generated'
openssl x509 -in /tmp/certs/node.crt -noout -subject -ext subjectAltName
" 2>&1
